target.name: Antora Energy
target.founder: Justin Briggs, Andrew Ponec, David Bierman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $150M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech peers across climate and energy, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $150M raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $285M. The peer-median method benchmarks against five same-sector, same-stage comparables: Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M). The median of this peer set is $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. This method implies $310M.

The two methods converge on a median valuation of $298M, with a defensible range of $242M to $357M. The stage-progression approach sits modestly below peer median, likely reflecting that raised capital captures only a portion of post-investment value creation at this stage. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for this specific sector's risk profile, and whether the peer set—dominated by hardware and infrastructure plays—fully captures Antora's technical differentiation and commercialization timeline. Dispersion within the peer group ($120M to $500M) indicates material heterogeneity in how investors are pricing comparable-stage climate ventures.
